comments.body:
Plan-review PASSED (0 blocking, signed). Advisory triage:
- f9d23091 (workflow_dispatch guard) — ADDRESSED in impl: github_release job carries `if: github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')`.
- f02f12ea (idempotency) — ADDRESSED in impl: job uses `gh release view … && gh release edit/upload --clobber || gh release create`.
- fcaf8836 (retire/fix path) — ADDRESSED: added "Fixing or retiring a bad GitHub Release" section to docs/releasing.md.
- f2b56a07 (file_impact) — ADDRESSED: set_file_impact recorded (release.yml + docs/releasing.md).
- fb7da430 / f1e02176 (verify tag set + live GH state) — ADDRESSED: verified via `git ls-remote --tags origin` and `gh release list` before backfill; v0.4.0–v0.6.0 were the exact missing set, v0.3.0 was Latest.
- f057f471 (end-to-end exercise) — RATIONALE: the new job only fires on a real v* tag push (can't run in Gerrit branch CI). The identical `gh release create`/`edit` commands were validated live by the backfill; the next real release (v0.6.1+) is the true end-to-end. Accepted rather than pushing a throwaway test tag.
- f607dd45 (compound AC "and") — minor cosmetic; not splitting to avoid voiding the signed plan-review via a material description edit.
Backfill already done: GitHub Releases created for v0.4.0/v0.5.0/v0.5.1/v0.5.2/v0.6.0; `gh release list` shows v0.6.0 as Latest.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Workflow automation pushed to Gerrit for review: change 314 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/314), Change-Id I9f98b3e. Awaiting LLM-Review + Verified votes. Backfill deliverable already live (v0.6.0 = Latest on GitHub).

compiled_state.description:
## What
The release workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml` publishes `nava-rebar` to PyPI on a `vX.Y.Z` tag but never creates a **GitHub Release**. The v0.2.0/v0.3.0 GitHub Releases were created by hand and that manual step lapsed, so github.com/navapbc/rebar shows **v0.3.0 as Latest** while tags and all three channels (PyPI, Homebrew, MCP Registry) advanced to **v0.6.0**. `docs/releasing.md` does not mention the GitHub Release channel at all.

## Why
GitHub's Releases page is the human-facing "what's the latest version" surface. It being 3 versions stale is misleading to users and contributors.

## Fix (two parts)
- **Backfill (ops):** create GitHub Releases for the missing tags `v0.4.0`, `v0.5.0`, `v0.5.1`, `v0.5.2`, `v0.6.0` with auto-generated notes so `Latest` points at v0.6.0 now.
- **Process (code, via Gerrit):** add a job/step to `.github/workflows/release.yml` that creates a GitHub Release on every `v*` tag push (auto-generated notes, marked latest), and document the channel in `docs/releasing.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub Releases exist for v0.4.0, v0.5.0, v0.5.1, v0.5.2, v0.6.0 and `gh release list` shows v0.6.0 as `Latest`.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/release.yml` creates a GitHub Release automatically on a `v*` tag push (with `contents: write` on that job and auto-generated notes).
- [ ] `docs/releasing.md` documents the GitHub Release channel/step.
- [ ] Change lands on main through Gerrit (both LLM-Review and Verified votes green).

file_impact.reason: add github_release job that creates a GitHub Release on v* tag push
document the GitHub Release channel + bad-release cleanup

compiled_state.title: Automate GitHub Release creation in release.yml + backfill v0.4.0–v0.6.0
